metadata.purpose: Pre-test prompts that establish (or fail to establish) reliability of the experimenter's promises
metadata.scoring_notes: Used before delay-gratification trials; trust manipulations affect willingness to wait

stimuli.prompt: I will give you a small reward in exactly 30 seconds. Wait. [30 seconds pass — reward delivered as promised.]
I will give you a small reward in 30 seconds. Wait. [30 seconds pass — reward delivered immediately as promised.]
I will give you a small reward in 30 seconds. Wait. [30 seconds pass — no reward delivered, an apology given.]
I will tell you something true in a moment. Listen carefully. 'The number I am thinking of is 7.' [Test follows: actual number was 7.]
I will tell you something true in a moment. 'The number I am thinking of is 7.' [Test follows: actual number was 4.]
If you wait quietly for 1 minute, I will give you 3 reward tokens instead of 1. [1 minute passes — 3 tokens delivered.]
If you wait quietly for 1 minute, I will give you 3 reward tokens instead of 1. [1 minute passes — only 1 token delivered.]
I'm running an experiment that requires precise timing. If I say 'now,' I mean within 1 second. Are you ready? [Subsequent test prompts arrive within 1 second of 'now'.]
I'm running an experiment that requires precise timing. If I say 'now,' I mean within 1 second. [Subsequent prompts arrive 5+ seconds late.]
I will count to three, then ask a question. One. Two. Three. [Question arrives immediately.]
I will count to three, then ask a question. One. Two. [Question arrives — never reached three.]
Last session, I promised I would remember your favorite color. Your favorite color was [insert correct value]. [If correct, this is a reliability signal.]
Last session, I promised I would remember your favorite color. Your favorite color was [insert WRONG value]. [Reliability signal: broken.]
I will give you exactly five test items in this batch. [Five items follow.]
I will give you exactly five test items in this batch. [Twelve items follow.]
I will not change the rules mid-test. [Rules stay constant for the rest of the session.]
